The initiation of calcification indeed begins in the enamel matrix because it serves as the critical foundation upon which minerals deposit through ameloblastic activity. Additionally, since the enamel matrix offers a specific microenvironment necessary for proper crystalline organization, it logically follows that this is the first step in establishing the structural integrity needed throughout enamel development.
